description: Remove one or more tags from an existing task

changes: Initial implementation for task tags feature

task_id.description: ID of the task to remove tags from. Must be owned by the current user.

tags.description: Array of tags to remove. Tags will be normalized to lowercase before removal. Non-existent tags are silently ignored.

task.description: Updated task object with tags removed

tags.description: Remaining tags on the task after removal

tags_removed.description: List of tags that were actually removed (excludes non-existent tags)

natural_language_examples.intent: Remove 'work' tag from task with ID 5
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Tag 'work' is removed from task 5. If task doesn't have 'work', operation succeeds but nothing changes.

natural_language_examples.user_input: remove tags urgent and high-priority from task 3
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Both tags are removed from task 3 (case-insensitive)

natural_language_examples.intent: Remove tags, but unclear which ones
natural_language_examples.note: Agent should ask: 'Which tags would you like to remove from task 1?'
Agent should first get task tags with list_tasks, then call remove_tag with all tags

error_handling.trigger: Task ID does not exist or not owned by user

response.message: Task with ID {task_id} not found or not accessible

response.note: Operation succeeds, but tags_removed array is empty or partial

performance_requirements.implementation_approach: Direct JSONB array filter in PostgreSQL

security_requirements: User isolation: Can only remove tags from own tasks

understanding_commands: User says 'remove tag X from task N' → remove_tag(task_id=N, tags=['X'])
User says 'untag task N with X, Y' → remove_tag(task_id=N, tags=['X', 'Y'])
User says 'remove all tags from task N' → First get current tags, then remove all
Case-insensitive: 'remove Work' and 'remove work' are identical
response_formatting: Confirm which tags were removed: 'Removed tags 'work', 'urgent' from task "{title}"'
If tags didn't exist: 'Task didn't have tags: {not_found_tags}'
Show remaining tags: 'Task now has tags: {remaining_tags}' or 'Task has no tags' if empty

operations: SELECT task with user_id and task_id (ownership check)
UPDATE task SET tags = filtered_tags (array filter)
